cross_scene_arcs.title: Nora's Relationship with Sheldon
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Nora reluctantly recruits Sheldon, conflicts over commercial content, recognizes his connection with kids, rejects him after a scandal, reconciles, faces danger together, protects him, and ends in a romantic relationship.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Reluctant executive assigned to recruit Sheldon Mopes
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Romantic partner with Sheldon, having reconciled and survived threats

cross_scene_arcs.title: Professional Integrity vs. Network Politics
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Nora enforces Stokes' commercial directives, but after seeing Sheldon's genuine impact, she exposes Stokes' plan to replace him, shifting from enforcer to ally.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Compliant network executive following Stokes' orders
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Opposing Stokes and supporting Sheldon against corruption
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Potential ongoing threats from network politics

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confrontation with External Dangers
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Nora is threatened by Chick, confronts armed Randolph, and later protects Sheldon in an alley shootout, moving from victim to active defender.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Threatened and passive in the face of danger
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Active participant in violent confrontations, protecting Sheldon
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The aftermath of the alley shooting and Burke's fate

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Desperation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Randolph's career collapses after a bribery sting, leading to homelessness, rejection by allies, and a public suicide threat.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Arrested and career in jeopardy after accepting a bribe.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Homeless and threatening self-immolation in Times Square.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Randolph will ignite himself or find a way out of his despair.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Rival to Partner with Sheldon
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Randolph's antagonism toward Smoochy evolves from stage disruption and deception to armed confrontation, reconciliation, a life-saving act, and finally a partnership performance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Publicly disrupting Smoochy's show by shouting about a cookie.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Performing with Sheldon in a sold-out benefit show, receiving applause.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Sheldon and Nora's Turbulent Connection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sheldon meets Nora Bishop, a Kidnet executive, and admires her. He attempts partnership, but she rejects him. After public accusations of neo-Nazi ties lead Nora to sever their relationship, they eventually rekindle physical intimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sheldon has established contact with Nora and is engaging with her.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sheldon and Nora are physically intimate again after overcoming emotional distance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The stability of their relationship given past rejections and scandals.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Sheldon's Battle for Creative Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Burke advises Sheldon that money can buy power over Smoochy. Sheldon gains executive producer status but rejects commercial deals, faces threats from Parade of Hope, asserts independent control over the ice show, and confronts Burke, sparing him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Burke offers Sheldon a path to gain control through financial means.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sheldon has taken full control of the ice show and spared Burke after a confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Burke's implied fate and the ongoing challenge of maintaining independence.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Sheldon's Ethical Stance and Non-Violence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sheldon protects Smoochy from commercial exploitation by tearing up a contract. He disarms Randolph, choosing friendship over violence, and ultimately hesitates to shoot Burke, handing over the gun and leaving with Nora.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sheldon takes a firm stance against selling out Smoochy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sheldon sheds the Smoochy costume and rejects violence, affirming his ethical principles.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The personal cost of his choices and the future direction of his career.
